title: STORAG ETZEL and EnBW Agree on Next Step for Large-Scale Hydrogen Storage at the Etzel Site
content: STORAG ETZEL GmbH and EnBW Etzel Speicher GmbH have signed a contract to develop and operate large-scale hydrogen cavern storage at the Etzel site, with over one million cubic meters of underground capacity. EnBW also plans a new above-ground hydrogen facility at the site. The Etzel H2 storage project, set to be H2-ready by 2027, aims to support the North Sea-Benelux to southwest Germany hydrogen corridor. The site currently has 75 caverns for oil and gas, with potential for 24 caverns to be converted for hydrogen. Two caverns are already in test operation, with each capable of storing 200-250 GWh of hydrogen.
author: Analysts Account
